Try HARD to REFUTE this proposed change to the POLARIS pipeline. Default refuted=true if uncertain. Read code at /home/polaris/wt/flywheel if needed.

CHANGE: Padding purge + shipping-defect hygiene (frees the word budget C1 spends) (C6)
FILE/SEAM: /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/src/polaris_graph/generator/provenance_generator.py:5030-5065 (render loop, for the metadata filter and hygiene), /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/src/polaris_graph/retrieval/contradiction_detector.py:2477 ('No contradictions detected in the evidence corpus.'), multi_section_generator.py:3760 (rule #9 citation-diversity) :: ~30-35% of added words are padding; vacuous metadata sentences ('received 239 Scopus citations') pass strict_verify because it checks grounding not informativeness; the unsatisfiable rule #9 makes the writer pad by restating; and the report ships 'No contradictions were detected by the pipeline' inside the section graded for critical synthesis.
WHAT: Four small gates, one env each, all default OFF: (1) PG_DROP_METADATA_SENTENCES — post-verify pre-render filter dropping sentences whose only content is citation-count/venue/volume metadata with no outcome claim (deterministic regex + no-outcome-number check; drops from RENDER only, kept in audit log so faithfulness telemetry is unchanged); (2) PG_OMIT_EMPTY_CONTRADICTION_STUB — when the detector finds nothing, emit NOTHING instead of the stub sentence; (3) PG_SECTION_RULE9_SOFT — replace the fixed distinct-source quota with 'cite as many DISTINCT sources as the evidence genuinely supports; NEVER restate an already-made point to reuse a citation'; (4) PG_RENDER_HYGIENE — fix the mid-word section starts and strip fact_dedup's internal cross-reference sentences ('...is detailed under <section>') from rendered prose.
CLAIMS: +0.3 to +0.8 directly; its main value is protective — it buys back ~800-1500 padding words so C1's synthesis section does not push us past reference length, where Rank7 showed length becomes strictly harmful. on readability (0.14) + insight (removes the anti-insight stub and the density dilution); enables C1 within the 9k-word envelope (w=0.6)
FAILURE MODE THEY ADMIT: (a) The metadata regex over-fires and deletes real findings that mention a journal name (interacts badly with C7's venue attribution — order the passes: venue attribution runs on SURVIVORS). (b) Softening rule #9 lets citation diversity collapse in evidence-rich sections. (c) Word count drops without the freed budget being refilled, replaying Rank8's regression.
NEW FAITHFULNESS CONTRACT: TWO-TIER ADMISSION CONTRACT. TIER F (FACT) — unchanged, byte-identical when all new flags are off: a sentence is admitted iff it clears every existing leg of verify_sentence_provenance (provenance_generator.py:2098-2959) and `is_verified = len(failures) == 0` (:2930). TIER I (INFERENCE, body, PG_STRICT_VERIFY_INFERENCE_TIER): a sentence reaching the NEUTRAL branch (:2596-2621) is admitted iff ALL of: (1) it cites >=2 distinct evidence rows via provenance tokens — untokened sentences still die at :2186-2193, unchanged; (2) every MECHANICAL leg already passed — these run before entailment and are untouched, so every number, percent, integer, and binding qualifier in the sentence is already verified against the cited spans; (3) the first judge verdict was NEUTRAL — CONTRADICTED remains unconditionally fatal (:2607-2615 posture preserved); (4) the sentence carries an interpretive hedge cue (_INTERPRETIVE_CUE_RE, analyst_synthesis_deviation_check.py:219-226) — a bare categorical assertion is ineligible; (5) no named entity (author, study, organization, venue) appears in the sentence that is absent from the union of the cited rows' full text (reusing _span_grounds_sentence:138-152 and the author-attribution floor at :264-302); (6) a SECOND judge call with a premise-decomposition prompt returns INFERENCE_OK, defined as: every factual assertion in the SENTENCE is present in the SPAN, and the only content not in the SPAN is the hedged interpretive relation over those assertions — any other verdict, and any transport error, FAILS CLOSED to the existing entailment_failed drop (:2919-2923 posture preserved). Every Tier-I keep is tagged soft_warning='inference_tier' in the audit log and counted in telemetry, so the audit can enumerate exactly which sentences carry interpretation. TIER I-S (analyst section, existing dark-lane contract, unchanged from its authors' design): cited synthesis passes the deterministic anti-fabrication floor (every number in the union of cited sources' full text, >=2 content-word overlap); uncited interpretation passes _is_disclosable_interpretation (no decimals, no percent, no quantitative claim, no attribution absent from the pool, hedge cue mandatory); fabricated attributions and unhedged categoricals DROP; the section ships under an explicit interpretive disclosure. WHAT STILL GETS KILLED, everywhere, in every tier: any sentence contradicted by its spans; any unhedged interpretive assertion; any sentence introducing a number, percent, or named entity not groundable in the union of its cited sources; any untokened body sentence; anything on judge transport error. WHY NO HALLUCINATION HOLE OPENS: the only novel content class admitted anywhere is a HEDGED INTERPRETIVE RELATION whose operands are each independently span-verified — numeric and entity fabrication remain blocked by deterministic legs that run BEFORE and independently OF the judge; the change converts exactly one verdict path (NEUTRAL + hedged + premise-verified) from drop to labeled-keep, and every such keep is individually enumerable in the audit log for post-hoc inspection.

ATTACK THROUGH THIS LENS:
THE PRIOR NEGATIVE + NOISE: effect must exceed a +/-0.016 noise floor at n=1. Is the claimed gain distinguishable? And is this genuinely different from the STEP-3 insight directive that already failed, or is it the same idea with new words?

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

# POLARIS OVERNIGHT PROGRESS (on-box driver, Opus 4.8)

Driver session started 2026-07-11 13:54 UTC. Operator asleep; trail lives here + in git.

## READ THIS FIRST AT WAKE-UP — two blockers changed the plan

**BLOCKER 1 — the three wheel worktrees are NOT writable by the box driver.**
`/workspace/compose_wt`, `/workspace/outline_agent_wt`, `/workspace/outline_tooluse_wt` are all
`root:root 755`. The box driver runs as uid 1000 (`polaris`) and has **no sudo**. Every wheel the
brief assigns me is therefore read-only to me, as are the root-owned processes running inside them.

Workaround (deviation from the brief, deliberate, and the operator should know):
I created polaris-owned worktrees from the **exact same commits**, one per wheel, so the
one-worktree-per-wheel rule still holds and the trail still lands in git:

| wheel | brief's worktree (root, read-only to me) | my worktree (writable) | branch | forked from |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| agentic-outline | /workspace/outline_agent_wt | /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent | bot/outline-agent-box | ecda022 |
| tool-use compute | /workspace/outline_tooluse_wt | /home/polaris/wt/tooluse | bot/outline-tooluse-box | ecda022 |
| compose depth | /workspace/compose_wt | /home/polaris/wt/compose | bot/compose-box | eff82fb |

Merging these back is a fast-forward-able branch merge; no work is stranded.

**BLOCKER 2 — "serve the compose model locally on the A100" is not physically possible on this box.**
The brief's compose unlock assumes we can serve the compose model locally to escape the OpenRouter
429 ceiling. The compose model is `z-ai/glm-5.2` (confirmed in the live logs). That is a ~355B-class
MoE. This box has:
- **114 GB free disk** — the FP8 weights alone are ~355 GB. They do not fit on disk, let alone VRAM.
- **80 GB free VRAM** (GPU0). GPU1 has 31.7 GB held by an **orphaned root-owned vLLM EngineCore**
  (PID 8846, PPID 1, no listening port, running since Jul 08). It is dead weight and I **cannot kill
  it** — it is root-owned and I have no sudo. Operator: `sudo kill -9 8846` reclaims 31.7 GB.
- vLLM is **not installed** in the box python (torch 2.5.1 + transformers 5.8.1 are).

So "serve GLM-5.2 locally" is off the table regardless of effort. What IS feasible locally is the
*verifier* side (a small NLI model), which is the high-call-volume, low-complexity half of the
workload. That is the honest version of the unlock and it is what I am pricing out.

**BLOCKER 3 — I cannot stop the running compose jobs.** Two root-owned `run_s5_i3.py` runs
(PIDs 958596, 966951) launched by the laptop at 12:55 and 13:08 are still hammering OpenRouter with
glm-5.2 and are **self-contending for the same rate limit** (15x HTTP 429 already in one log). I can
neither kill nor deprioritize them. They will keep distorting any compose timing I measure.

## Time budget
Driver window is ~13:54 -> ~15:00 UTC = **~65 minutes**, not a full night. Scope is triaged
accordingly: I will not pretend to land a 34-tool toolkit + 22-case battery + local serving stack in
an hour. Priority is (1) truth on the record, (2) the single highest-value wheel actually landed and
independently gated.

## HEADLINE — the two wheels were each bricked at ONE LINE, not missing features

The single most important thing the operator should read this morning:

**1. The compose ceiling was NEVER the OpenRouter 429. It was a self-inflicted serialization.**
`_draft_passes_wrapper` (`generator/abstractive_writer.py:661`) is a plain sync `def`. It was called
with **no `await`** from inside the async coroutine `_pre_pass_one_basket` (`:749`), at two call
sites. It loops sentences -> `writer_verify_fn` -> `provenance_generator.verify_sentence_provenance`
-> `entailment_judge.judge()` -> `_post_with_total_deadline` -> `fut.result(timeout=total_s)` — which
**blocks the calling thread**. The calling thread is the asyncio event loop.

So **every NLI judge POST froze the entire compose event loop.** Achieved verify concurrency was
**1**, regardless of `PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER_CONCURRENCY=12` or `PG_MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM=16`. The
"~48-way parallel" compose was 48-way *only between* verifications. Every writer call in every
section stalled behind each single NLI POST.

This reframes the whole compose thesis in the brief. We were tuning 429 backoff and pricing out a
$355GB local serving stack to escape a rate limit that **was not the binding constraint.**

FIXED (`bot/compose-box` @ `0615bc5`): both call sites now `await asyncio.to_thread(...)`.
**Fable gate: SIGN_OFF, 0 P0/P1 — with an empirical A/B probe**, not just a code read:

| | elapsed (4 baskets) | verify thread | event loop |
|---|---|---|---|
| HEAD | 2.06s (serialized) | `MainThread` | frozen; heartbeat starved throughout |
| fixed | 0.60s (parallel) | `asyncio_0..3` | live; max heartbeat gap 0.050s |

The judge machinery was **already built for this** and had been waiting: `judge_verdict_cache` is
`RLock`-guarded and its own docstring says it is "shared across the ThreadPool verifier workers";
the judge holds per-thread httpx clients via `threading.local`; POSTs are bounded by a
`BoundedSemaphore`. All of it was moot because of one missing `await`.

**2. The compute stack is not dormant — it is advertised and guaranteed to fail.**
`outline_agent.py:1579` hardcoded `client=None` into every tool dispatch. `execute_python` is
registered, is `requires_llm=True`, and IS listed to the decide LLM as available — and returns
`success=False, "No LLM client available"` (`tool_registry.py:556`) on **100% of calls**. It was not
in `_tool_failure_gap_check`'s watched set either, so the failure was **silent**: the agent moved on
and the number got written from evidence prose instead of computed.

PARTIALLY fixed (`bot/outline-tooluse-box` @ `1b2e3c7`) — **this wheel is NOT signed off**, see below.

## NEXT ACTIONS, in priority order (start here)

1. **[compose] Tune `PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY` against a measured run.** The `to_thread` fix
   makes this knob *live on the compose path* for the first time — it is now the binding constraint
   on the verify phase (default **4**). Raising it is the actual throughput unlock for "render all
   346 baskets fast". I did **NOT** raise it blind: the default is deliberate storm protection, the
   judge shares the GLM-5.2 model *and the OpenRouter account* with the writer, and I could not get a
   clean measurement while the root-owned jobs hammered the same rate limit. Needs one clean A/B.
   (Correction to my own earlier claim: this knob was NOT a global no-op — it was already binding on
   the credibility/strict-verify `to_thread` paths. It was non-binding *only* on the compose path.)
2. **[tooluse] The computed value is still UNREACHABLE.** This is the open Fable P1 and the reason

151 FLYWHEEL_PROGRESS.md
55:- 2026-07-12 | Rank6 KILLED — **THE DEPTH CEILING IS COMPOSE, NOT RETRIEVAL** | Fable's decisive finding, verified by me against the source: word count is architecturally fixed and **evidence-independent**. `multi_section_generator.py:4` states the contract outright — *"produces 1500-3000-word reports"* — and the section writer's rule 8 (:3564, :3713) sets a **fixed** *"Target 10-18 sentences"* per section regardless of payload. 11 sections x 10-18 sentences x ~22 words = **2.4-4.4k words = exactly our observed ~4k.** Pushing MORE rows into sections (what Rank6 would have done) is the direction this project **already measured as WORSE**: WHEEL_PROGRESS.md:259-261 — *"THIN body (2141 words vs step3 3230) ... kept_fraction=0.35 -> 102 dropped sentences (step3=64), 77 verified (step3=91)"*. More rows drowned strict_verify and made the report SHORTER. **Rank6 was aimed at the wrong stage.**
65:- 2026-07-12 21:50 | ALSO: kept_fraction=0.35 was a **MENU-SIZE** artifact, not a topicality one — multi_section_generator.py:513-527 records it was measured with PG_ROUTE_ALL_BASKETS + PG_EV_BUDGET_TRACKS_PAYLOAD ON, which ballooned section menus to **52-103 rows** (healthy step3 = ~15); the writer was crammed and 65% of what it wrote failed span-binding. **The purpose-built fix, `PG_WRITER_TOPN_EV_PER_SECTION` (:541), caps the writer's prompt menu and has NEVER been switched on — it appears only in tests.** Our compose script sets both of the flags that caused the collapse (compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py:530-531) and does not set the cap.
112:- 2026-07-13 00:45 | **UNPLANNED GIFT: Rank10 is now a REPLICATION of Rank9** (theme floor = no-op => identical effective config). Every arm tonight has been **n=1**, and I have twice had to reason about noise (the +0.085 kept_fraction, the word-count spread) **without any variance estimate at all**. This run supplies one for free. **I will read Rank10 as a variance probe, not as a new arm** — and any Rank9-vs-Rank10 gap is the noise floor against which every claim tonight must be re-read.
115:  - **WITHDRAWN: "the cap raised density 12.52 -> 13.84."** That is **+1.32 against a measured noise floor of 1.70** — inside the noise. I cannot distinguish it from run-to-run variance and I am not going to pretend otherwise. (The stacked cap's kept_fraction gain, 0.512 -> 0.562/0.572 against ±0.01 noise, **does** survive — the cap is real, my *reason* for liking it was not.)
118:- 2026-07-13 01:35 | **WHAT SURVIVES THE NOISE FLOOR — THE REAL SCORECARD.** Baseline -> best (Rank10): **words 3408 -> 9300 (2.7x)**, **verified sentences 81 -> 219 (2.7x, on the STABLE metric)**, **distinct works cited 42 -> 94 (2.2x)**, kept_fraction 0.519 -> 0.572, sections 10 -> 10. Density fell 16.88 -> 12.14 (-28%, beyond noise — **real, and the honest cost of the depth push**). **Frontier floor is 10k words; we are at 9.3k.** Levers that got us here, both of which were **already in the code and switched off**: `PG_SECTION_SENTENCE_TARGET` (Rank7, built tonight at the one prompt seam) + `PG_WRITER_TOPN_EV_PER_SECTION` (pre-existing, never once enabled).
135:  **Rank9/10 are the SAME config => their 0.0158 gap IS the noise floor. Rank11 sits INSIDE it. Ranks 9/10/11 mean ~0.42 and are statistically indistinguishable.** Baseline 0.4052 -> best ~0.42 = **+0.015, about ONE noise-width, for the whole night's 2.7x depth push.** Quoting Rank10's 0.4313 as "our score" is cherry-picking the luckiest draw. **The 2.7x words / 2.7x verified sentences / 2.2x distinct works headline DID NOT BUY SCORE.**
143:- 2026-07-13 03:00 | **NEXT STEP — FIX THE INSTRUMENT BEFORE TOUCHING THE CODE. Both reviewers converged on this independently and it is the most important thing either said: EVERY lever in both plans is SMALLER THAN OUR MEASUREMENT NOISE.** We have always scored n=1 against a +/-0.016 noise floor while chasing effects worth ~+0.010. **We cannot see our own results.** (1) Score a byte-identical report k=5 to pin the true per-call SD. (2) Then ONE bundled arm — structural reflow (H3s, 100-200w topic-sentence paragraphs, a study table) + in-place hedged interpretation — over Rank10's FROZEN evidence assignments at ZERO added words, scored 5x paired (SE ~0.008). I have what I need. Let me lay out the refutation.

**Evidence gathered (all from /home/polaris/wt/flywheel and /home/polaris/polaris_project/FLYWHEEL_PROGRESS.md):**

1. **The exact experiment has already been run and came back null.** Rank11 (cross-section repetition guard) deleted ~2,000 words of *real* padding: 9,194 → 7,178 words. OVERALL 0.4313 → 0.4133; readability 0.3774 → 0.3802 (+0.003). Log line: *"The padding was real and it was worth nothing — but removing it was also worth nothing. Clean cheap negative."* C6 proposes to remove 800–1,500 words — **less than half of what Rank11 already removed for zero effect.**

2. **The named padding classes barely exist in the shipped text.** Scanning bodies of the actual reports: citation-count metadata sentences (`received N citations`, `Scopus citations`, `has been cited`) = **0 hits in rank9, rank10, rank12**; **1 hit in rank7** — which is the single instance the proposal quotes ("received 239 Scopus citations"). `"is detailed under"` cross-reference sentences = **0 hits**. The contradiction stub = **1 clause (~25 words)** inside the Limitations line. Total addressable: ~25–50 words, not 800–1,500. The "30–35% of added words are padding" figure has no measurement behind it.

3. **The metadata regex's false-positive surface is larger than its true-positive surface.** A broad metadata scan of rank12 returns 5 sentences; 4 are substantive content ("One systematic review guided by PRISMA 2020 searched Scopus and Web of Science…", the worker-inclusion finding). True positives ≈ 0.

4. **The protective premise is false.** Scored arms: Rank10 (9,194w) = 0.4313, the **highest**; Rank8 (4,226w) = 0.3957, the **lowest**. There is no measured length penalty, and Rank7's low score (0.4041) is not attributable to length since Rank9/10 are longer and score higher. Post-cleaner reference ≈ 59 × 142 ≈ **8.4k words** vs our 7.7k — we are already **below** reference length. There is no budget crunch for C1 to be protected from.

5. **The freed budget is smaller than the noise in the quantity it protects.** Identical config, two runs: 8,055 vs 9,300 words (±15%; body ±22%). You cannot reserve 800–1,500 words in a system whose word count swings ±1,245 at fixed config.

6. **Claimed effect vs noise floor.** +0.3 to +0.8 points = +0.003–0.008 on the 0–1 scale, against a measured ±0.016 noise floor at n=1 (Rank9/Rank10 identical-config gap = 0.0158). The claim is **20–50% of one noise width** — undetectable, and the rubric caps it anyway: redundancy/density is graded under readability P1 (w=0.15 inside readability's 0.14 = **0.021 of total**); even a perfect +0.2 absolute move on that criterion yields ≈ +0.004.

7. **Rule #9 softening is the one part with real (negative) leverage**: it mutates the writer prompt of the only config with scored baselines (tier-first + cap30 + distinct15) and puts distinct-works — one of only two metrics measured as *stable* — at risk, in exchange for an unmeasurable readability gain, and it confounds C1's arm if run together.

The only genuine item is a bug: **2 of 11 sections in rank12 ship with truncated first words** ("ceptible to potential artificial intelligence impacts…", "d and the specific skill sets involved.[82]"). That is a real, judge-visible prose corruption — but it is a byte-level fix, not a lever, and does not need a flag, an env, or a scored run.
